ai_scope_discriminator: The in-scope gate is the snapshot SOURCE PATH, not the server itself. GET /api/v1/sources returns a `sources[]` array where each element's `source` object carries `{userName, host, path}`. Read those `path` values (names only) and confirm the backup actually targets AI/ML data before treating it as Cat-13 in-scope: paths under MLflow artifact stores (`/mlruns`, `.../mlflow/artifacts`, `mlartifacts/`), HuggingFace caches (`~/.cache/huggingface`, `hub/models--*`), model checkpoint dirs (`*.ckpt`, `*.safetensors`, `*.gguf`, `checkpoints/`, `models/`), vector-DB data dirs (`.../chroma`, `qdrant/storage`, `milvus`, `weaviate`, `lancedb`, `*.faiss`), or training-data/dataset roots (`datasets/`, `*.parquet` corpora, `.dvc`). A Kopia server whose only sources are `/home`, `/etc`, Docker volumes, or generic DB dumps is plain substrate (file/DB backup) and is OUT of Cat-13 scope per the AI-scope-not-substrate rule. Cross-check: a source `path` ending in an MLflow/vector-DB/model dir + the existence of those snapshots in /api/v1/snapshots = confirmed AI/ML backup repository.
fingerprint_markers: JSON body of GET /api/v1/sources contains the exact field quartet: "localUsername", "localHost", "multiUser", and a "sources" array
Each /api/v1/sources element has SourceStatus fields "source","status","schedule","lastSnapshot","nextSnapshotTime","upload","currentTask" (vendor-unique combination)

GET /api/v1/repo/status StatusResponse fields: "connected","configFile","formatVersion","hash","encryption","ecc","splitter","maxPackSize","storage" (hash/encryption/splitter trio is Kopia-format-unique)
Web UI: HTML title / React bundle branded "KopiaUI"; default self-signed TLS cert on :51515
Unauthenticated request to a protected route returns body "Missing credentials."; with creds but no token returns "Invalid or missing CSRF token." (both Kopia-server-unique error strings)
fp_guards: The legacy `kopia server` default (no --without-password) is auth-ON: probes return HTTP 401 with body "Missing credentials." or "Invalid or missing CSRF token." — surface open, access NOT exercised; do NOT label these unauth. Only --without-password --insecure servers return a real 200 sources/snapshots body.

LBot / catch-all deception pages can echo arbitrary JSON; require the EXACT field quartet localUsername+localHost+multiUser+sources AND a well-formed SourceStatus element, not just the substring "sources". A catch-all that 200s every path is the negative guard.

Broad http.title or generic "backup" dorks pull in Restic REST server, Bacula, UrBackup, Duplicati, BorgBackup web UIs on :51515-adjacent ports — exclude by requiring the SourcesResponse JSON field names, which none of those emit.
Port 51515 is not IANA-reserved for Kopia; unrelated services bind it. Confirm with the /api/v1/repo/status hash+encryption+splitter trio before attributing to Kopia.
joachimdalen/kopia-alternate-ui and other drop-in UIs proxy the same API — fingerprint the API JSON (vendor truth), not the HTML chrome, to avoid mislabeling the front-end as the platform.
A 200 on the web UI index alone is not unauth API access; the load-bearing read is a 200-with-data on /api/v1/sources returning a populated sources[] array.

cves.desc: Unauthenticated RCE (CVSS 9.8) on POST /api/v1/repo/exists: SFTP storage-config fields are passed unsanitized into an OpenSSH command line (naive space-split, no tokenizer/quote handling), enabling -oProxyCommand=<cmd> injection. Exploitable only when server runs --without-password; affects <=0.22.3, fixed 0.23.0.

pivot_paths: GET /api/v1/repo/status -> read repo format metadata (storage backend type, hash/encryption/splitter, formatVersion) to confirm it is a live Kopia repo, names/config only
GET /api/v1/sources -> enumerate every backup source: list the source[].path values to apply the AI-scope discriminator (which paths are MLflow/vector-DB/model/dataset dirs)

GET /api/v1/snapshots?userName=&host=&path=

-> list snapshot manifests (timestamps, sizes, root object IDs) for an in-scope source; metadata only, never resolve the root
GET /api/v1/cli and GET /api/v1/current-user -> confirm passwordless/anonymous mode and server identity
GET /api/v1/tasks and /api/v1/tasks-summary -> read backup-job history/cadence for operator attribution, read-only
restraint_note: Names and metadata only. The in-scope finding is the existence of an unauth Kopia server whose snapshot SOURCE PATHS point at AI/ML data — that source-path list IS the finding. STOP there. Do NOT call GET /api/v1/objects/{objectID} or mount/restore endpoints to resolve a snapshot's contents: reading a snapshot tree or object = exfiltration of backed-up data. Do NOT POST to /api/v1/restore, /api/v1/mounts, /api/v1/repo/connect, /api/v1/repo/create, or /api/v1/repo/exists — restore/mount are write/read-out primitives and /api/v1/repo/exists is the CVE-2026-45695 RCE sink; any POST there is an active exploit, not enumeration. Enumerate the catalog, never the cargo.
